title: Kerala Raw Banana Puzhukku (Kappa-Style)

ingredients.name: raw banana (vazhakkai/nenthrappazham unripe), peeled and cut in chunks

subs.name: tapioca (kappa/cassava) cut in 3 cm cubes for the original kappa puzhukku
subs.notes: tapioca takes 20 minutes to boil and requires thorough cooking for food safety

subs.name: desiccated coconut moistened with 2 tbsp water
subs.notes: fresh coconut gives a far more vibrant result

steps.text: Place the raw banana chunks in a pot with turmeric, salt and enough water to cover. Boil on medium-high flame for 12–15 minutes until the banana is completely soft — a knife should slide through with zero resistance. Drain, reserving 2 tablespoons of the cooking water.
Grind the grated coconut with green chillies and cumin seeds to a coarse paste in a mixie — add just a tablespoon of water to keep it moving. The paste should be chunky, not fully smooth.

Mash the cooked banana coarsely with a fork or your fingers (traditional style) — leave some chunks for texture, do not make it smooth. Mix in the coconut-chilli paste along with the reserved cooking water. The mixture should hold together loosely.

Heat coconut oil in a kadai on medium-high. Splutter mustard seeds, add dried red chillies and curry leaves and let them crackle for 15 seconds. Add the chopped shallots and fry for 2 minutes until softened and golden.

Add the banana-coconut mixture to the kadai. Toss everything together on medium flame for 2 minutes until well combined and heated through. Check salt. Serve hot as a side dish with any Kerala curry and rice, or eat as a standalone breakfast with pickle.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: raw-banana → tapioca (kappa/cassava) cut in 3 cm cubes for the original kappa puzhukku (tapioca takes 20 minutes to boil and requires thorough cooking for food safety); coconut → desiccated coconut moistened with 2 tbsp water (fresh coconut gives a far more vibrant result); shallot → 1 small regular onion, finely chopped (shallots give sweeter flavour)
